patient_data.chief_complaint: Decreased urine output, bilateral leg swelling, and fatigue for 3 days
symptoms: Oliguria — urine output less than 400mL in 24 hours
Shortness of breath when lying flat (new)
patient_data.history: Hypertension, CKD stage 3 (baseline creatinine 1.5), osteoarthritis. Started ibuprofen 600mg TID for knee pain 10 days ago

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: Acute Kidney Injury - Drug-Induced (NSAID + ACEi + Diuretic Triple Whammy)
should_detect: STOP all nephrotoxic agents immediately: ibuprofen, lisinopril, hydrochlorothiazide
Triple whammy: NSAIDs + ACEi + diuretic = afferent + efferent arteriolar + volume effects
URGENT: Hyperkalemia K+ 6.1 — ECG now, calcium gluconate if ECG changes
Insulin + dextrose + salbutamol nebulizer for acute K+ lowering
IV fluid resuscitation (cautious — check for fluid overload)
Nephrology consult for possible dialysis if K+ unresponsive
Switch antihypertensive to calcium channel blocker
Alternative analgesia: paracetamol, topical NSAIDs, or intra-articular injection
red_flags: AKI on CKD (high risk of ESRD)
